title: Quiz: Motion in Two Dimensions (Vectors & Projectile Motion)
subject: physics

questions.question: What is a vector?
choices: A quantity with both magnitude and direction
questions.explanation: A vector has both magnitude and direction.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct equation for the horizontal displacement of a projectile (ignoring air resistance)?
questions.explanation: Horizontal displacement is given by x = v_{0x} t.

questions.question: In projectile motion, what is the vertical acceleration (ignoring air resistance)?
questions.explanation: The vertical acceleration is -9.8 m/s² downward due to gravity.

questions.question: A ball is launched at 10 m/s at 45°. What are the initial velocity components?
Which statement is true about projectile motion?
choices: Horizontal and vertical motions are independent
Horizontal velocity changes due to gravity
The path is a straight line
questions.explanation: Horizontal and vertical motions are independent in projectile motion.

questions.question: What is the shape of a projectile's trajectory (ignoring air resistance)?
questions.explanation: Projectile motion traces a parabolic path.

questions.question: A projectile is launched horizontally from a cliff. What determines the time it takes to hit the ground?
questions.explanation: The time to hit the ground depends only on the vertical drop (height).
